description: Add the ability to reorder pages.
title: Add page reordering capability

descriptionHistory.description: Add the ability to reorder pages.

summary:
## Summary: Add the ability to reorder pages

### Changes Implemented
- Created a batch reorder API endpoint (`POST /api/pages/reorder`) that accepts an array of page IDs with new sort orders, validates workspace ownership, and updates all pages in a single database transaction — following the exact same pattern as the existing `blocks/reorder` endpoint
- Added a `useReorderPages` React Query mutation hook that calls the new API endpoint and invalidates the pages cache on success
- Added drag-and-drop reordering to the `PagesSidebar` using `@dnd-kit/core` and `@dnd-kit/sortable` (already installed in the project), including:
  - A grip/drag handle icon that appears on hover for each page item
  - `DndContext` with `PointerSensor` (5px activation constraint) and `KeyboardSensor`
  - `SortableContext` with `verticalListSortingStrategy` for root-level pages
  - `DragOverlay` showing the dragged page with elevated styling
  - Visual feedback: dragged item becomes transparent, overlay shows card-like styling with shadow
  - On drag end, computes new sort orders via `arrayMove` and calls `useReorderPages` mutation
- Children pages within expanded parents retain their existing sort order (non-draggable nested items maintain the existing behavior)

### Files Modified
- `app/api/pages/reorder/route.ts` — **NEW** batch reorder API endpoint
- `lib/hooks/usePages.ts` — Added `useReorderPages` mutation hook
- `components/pages/PagesSidebar.tsx` — Added dnd-kit drag-and-drop with grip handles, drag overlay, and sortable page items

### Notes for Developer
- The `sortOrder` column and index (`idx_pages_workspace_parent_sort`) already existed in the schema — no migration needed
- The reorder API supports an optional `parent_id` field per page, allowing future reparenting via drag (not exposed in UI yet but the API supports it)
- Only root-level pages are drag-sortable in the current UI; child pages keep their existing sort behavior
- The `@dnd-kit/core`, `@dnd-kit/sortable`, and `@dnd-kit/utilities` packages were already project dependencies
- TypeScript type checking passes (`tsc --noEmit` exits cleanly)
